translation: “And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice.”

sunni.label: Sunni emphasis
sunni.body: Sunni tafsir reads the ‘great’ sacrifice as the ram God sent in his place — a mercy beyond doubt accepted, founding the eternal rite of sacrifice.

shia.label: Shia emphasis
shia.body: Shia narrations read ‘great’ beyond the ram — foreshadowing the supreme sacrifice to come: Imam al-Ḥusayn at Karbala, honouring Ibrahim’s wish to give his dearest.

voiceover.hook: A father, in a dream, told to sacrifice his own son — and both submit. But the 'great' sacrifice God sends in his place, two traditions read very differently.
voiceover.reference: Surah as-Saaffaat, verse one hundred and seven, revealed in Mecca.
voiceover.question: Which of these two readings resonates with you

cta_text: Which of these two readings resonates with you?
caption_desc: What makes a sacrifice 'great'? Two traditions, two answers 🌙 #quran #tafsir #shia #sunni
